Hi, my name is Natalie Emery and I am applying for the CTE SME position. This video captures my responses to the three questions provided. Question one. There was one particular time that I built a CTE course beyond just reaching it. I was asked to teach a construction technology course due to high enrollment and a lack of instructors. So I was apprehensive to teach this course because the existing teacher did not follow course standards. They basically taught it based on their industry experience and had done the same thing over and over again. So there was no curriculum for me to follow. I had to start from scratch. Creating the lectures for this course wasn't the hard part. It was helping students make real-world connections with the content that really challenged me. So a couple of the things I did was found construction projects in the local area that I featured in our discussions. And so we would do critiques where we would talk about the purpose. We would analyze the design, like talk about the pros and cons just to bring awareness to the students about things going on around them in the community. I also partnered with the city government and local contractors and builders to arrange field trips for my students to tour construction sites hands-on so that they could see the different stages in the process of construction and ask questions about it. The last thing I did was invited guest speakers into the classroom to speak to students about the areas where I had no expertise. So those were the ways of partnering with the community was key for me in that course. The other thing was funding. Since I'd never taught the course before, I really had no idea what materials to buy for the course. And since the students love to build things, I found it very challenging to buy all the materials I needed because I didn't buy them in time. So what we ended up doing was networking with, again, the builder. I asked the builder to sponsor us for a project to build like a shed, a playhouse to sell for a fundraiser. And they agreed to donate all the materials. We also partnered with the student government association at the school to build some backdrops, partitions that they wanted for a dance. And so they funded us for those materials. Students passed safety tests in this course, but they did not pass any recognized credentials for the course. Looking back, it would be beneficial to have a certification like the NCCER Core in Construction Technology certification, but in order to add certifications in our district, we had to get approval and funding from CTE supervisors, which wasn't always easy to do. Second question. One pathway I know deeply is arts, audio, visual technology, and communications. This pathway encompasses all the ways that humans communicate with each other and includes videography, photography, graphic design, web design, and animation. To come up to speed on a course and a pathway outside my expertise, I would first find and review the course standards. And then once I found those, I would review the learning outcomes associated with each of them to get a better understanding of the scope of the curricular content. Next I would look and see if there's a student-sponsored association associated with the class and then look for certification tests. And the last thing would to actually like look at the materials themselves, the recommended curricular materials to gain more knowledge about that particular course. Question three. If I was handed a drafted CTE course to review, I would first locate and review the suggested curriculum materials associated with the course. And once I found those, I would check to make sure that they're aligned with the standards and take notes of any standards not included in the materials. Next I would make sure to look at the assignments, assessments, and projects, make sure that they are mapped to the standards. Next I would make sure that participation in the student associations related to the course, they're integrated into the standards. And I would evaluate the certification tests to map them to make sure that all the skills on the tests are mapped into the standards and the curriculum. Then I would review the financial requirements of everything that needs to be purchased to ensure that Perkins funding guidelines are in compliance. And then last I would document, you know, make sure that all of the gaps are documented clearly and concisely so that the writers can fill in the gaps. Thank you so much for this opportunity and I hope to hear from you soon. And thank you so much. Have a great day.
